name: Drenthe (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the field of the historic arms
the colour of the six stars representing the dingspelen
the colour of the castle, the historic seat of the prefect

description: A white field charged in the centre with a black castle keep surrounded by six red six-pointed stars in a circle.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 19 February 1947. The charges are taken directly from the historic arms of Drenthe: the castle representing the prefect's seat and the six stars the six dingspelen of the province.

features.symbolism: the white field provides a clear ground for the heraldic charges drawn from the historic provincial arms
the six historic Drenthe dingspelen (administrative regions): Noordenveld, Oostermoer, Zuidenveld, Beilerdingspel, Dieverderdingspel and Rolderdingspel
the castle (keep) representing the seat of the prefect of Drenthe
